personas.name: Tenant Admin
personas.description: A business owner or operations manager who onboards their organisation onto AnjiSchedulo. They configure business hours, build a service catalogue, manage their staff roster, set cancellation policies, and monitor daily performance via the tenant dashboard.
goals: See today's schedule and revenue at a glance
Manage staff availability without back-and-forth messages
frustrations: Manual scheduling via phone or spreadsheet
Hard to see which services are most popular
Staff calling in sick with no easy rebooking flow
primaryActions: View and manage the daily booking dashboard

personas.name: Staff Member
personas.description: An employee or contractor whose availability is managed on the platform. They receive booking confirmations, cancellation notices, and rescheduling alerts. They can block their own availability for holidays or personal time.
goals: Know their schedule without calling the front desk
Get notified immediately when a booking is made or cancelled
Block personal time without admin involvement
frustrations: No way to block unavailability without calling admin
Schedule changes communicated via WhatsApp groups

personas.name: End Customer
personas.description: The person booking an appointment with a tenant's business. They browse available slots, select a service and staff member, complete payment where required, and receive lifecycle communications.
goals: Book an appointment in under 2 minutes
Know exactly who they are seeing and when
frustrations: Calling during business hours only to find no availability
Showing up to find the appointment was never booked

personas.name: Platform Operator
personas.description: An internal AnjiSchedulo team member responsible for infrastructure health, incident response, and compliance operations. They monitor dead letter queues, triage unprocessable events, trigger data replays, and manage tenant lifecycle.
goals: Know instantly when any service degrades
Rebuild corrupted views without data loss
frustrations: No way to inspect failed events
primaryActions: Monitor DLQ depth and triage failed events
